---
title: "Reflection"
---

<style>
body {
  background-image: url('images/background.jpg');
  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-attachment: fixed;
}
</style>

## My Journey

This module introduced me to tools and workflows I hadn't used before, Quarto, Git, GitHub Pages, and building a website from scratch using code rather than a pre-built template. At first, configuring _quarto.yml and understanding how all the pieces fit together felt overwhelming, but utilizing online guides and supports, made me realize that Quarto was an easy and efficient way of creating a website.

## Technical Skills

Learning Git was one of the most valuable takeaways. Updating and keeping track of my projects through the remote repository are skills I will use beyond this module. Working with Quarto also gave me a deeper appreciation for reproducible documents, combining markdown, Python code, and web publishing into one workflow.

## Design Choices

I wanted my portfolio to feel modern yet be a reflection of me as a person. I chose a dark theme with a calming background image and translucent content boxes. Customising the SCSS variables and writing my own CSS rules taught me how web styling works at a practical level. Small details like hover effects, rounded borders, and a blurred navigation bar made a noticeable difference in the overall feel.

## Challenges

The biggest challenge was getting things to work consistently across pages, image paths, background images, and CSS selectors all behaved differently depending on folder depth.

## Connecting to Future Goals

Building this portfolio ties directly into my career goals in analytics and technology. Having a professional online presence where I showcase some of my projects, executable code, with a clear narrative is something I can continue to develop and share with potential employers. The technical skills this module required mirrors what I want to do professionally, turning complex work into something easy to understand.
